Tests if `Number.prototype` has a `toString` property that is a function.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion that checks the `Number` prototype in JavaScript. It verifies that the `Number.prototype` object has a property named `toString` and that this property is a function. This assertion ensures that the `toString` method, which is used to convert a number to a string representation, is correctly implemented and available on all `Number` objects. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on the integrity of the `toString` method within the `Number` prototype.
